nodes.label: 🏫 School of AI — OpenClaw Conversational AI System Playbook — v6.0
OpenClaw Conversational AI System — Complete AI-Facing Setup Playbook
⚖️ RULES OF ENGAGEMENT (READ THIS BEFORE TOUCHING ANYTHING)
When to use the subagent pattern (default: YES)
Spawning the subagent (main agent's only Phase 0 task)
O.1 — Invoke the Teach Yourself Protocol on this document
O.2 — Locate (or, last resort, create) the client's master files folder
Step O.2.A — Semantic discovery (read-only — no writes yet)
Step O.2.B — Disambiguate if multiple matches
Step O.2.C — Last resort: create a new folder
Step O.2.D — Save the playbook + companion files
Step O.2.E — Append to Run Manifest
O.3 — Detect the OpenClaw version and verify the schema is current
O.4 — Execute the Backup Protocol before any config edit
O.7 — Seed durable design principles into MEMORY.md
📋 How the operator uses this playbook
Pre-flight checklist (the agent must verify all items before proceeding)
Step 1 — Create the Cloudflare tunnel via API
Step 2 — Install cloudflared as a persistent system service
✅ Checkpoint B — Tunnel verified end-to-end (Phase 1 complete)
Step 3 — Configure OpenClaw's `hooks.mappings` for GHL inbound
Step 3.5 — Model selection wizard (REAL-TIME vs ASYNC tier)
A. Show the cost-vs-speed advisory FIRST
B. Ask the operator about REAL-TIME channels
C. Ask the operator about ASYNC channels
D. Ask about the batch/summarization model
E. Verify API keys for chosen providers
F. Verify the chosen models exist in OpenClaw's catalog
G. Write the model preferences into the hooks.mappings entry
H. Set up the 30-day System Health Heartbeat cron
I. Surface model config in the final summary
Step 4 — End-to-end test through the public tunnel
✅ Checkpoint C — OpenClaw responds to inbound webhooks (Phase 2 complete)
Step 5 — Save secrets to the env file
Step 6 — Generate the Client Reference Sheet
Step 6.5 — MANDATORY: deliver the doc LINK to the client via Telegram (un-skippable, state-gated)
✅ Checkpoint D — Operator has copy-paste materials (Phase 3 complete)
Step 7 — Install the inbound message classification rules into AGENTS.md
Step 8 — Scaffold the eight channel communication playbooks in Notion
Step 9 — Set up the conversation log system
A. Create the conversational logs folder
B. Write the conversation log protocol document
C. Update AGENTS.md to reference the conversation log protocol
D. Schedule the daily summarization cron job
E. Tell the operator what was set up
Update AGENTS.md to reference the safeguards
Add the safeguards check to the System Health Heartbeat (Step 3.5H)
✅ Checkpoint E — Agent is safety-ready and memory-ready (Phase 4 complete)
Step 9.12 — Install Conversation Export on Customer Request
Step 9.19 — Install Smart Booking System + Calendar Setup Wizard
Step 9.20 — Install Conversation Workflow Builder (3-Layer Architecture)
How this differs from Communication Playbooks (Step 8)
A. Trigger phrases — how the operator invokes the builder
B. Subagent walks the operator through INITIAL workflow creation
D.1 — Auto-create required tags via the GHL skill
D.3 — Generate the brutally-specific verification checklist
F.5 — Build-with-AI is PRIMARY. Manual node-building is the FALLBACK.
F.6 — Reusable Tunnel Values (BINDING — store every time, keep updated)
F.7 — The base SMS automation ALSO creates the first conversation playbook (appointment booking) and wires the hook to it
G. Initial workflow creation during setup
📐 THE TRINITY — three artifacts, one build (authoritative)
3. STORAGE — where the playbook FILE lives (master-files folder)
4. STORAGE ORDER — the human-readable copy in the CLIENT's account (fallback chain)
5. Distinction — communications playbook vs channel communication playbook
2. MUST-APPEAR checklist — every workflow-AI instruction set
3. Custom Webhook — explicit field-by-field (Build-with-AI fumbles these)
6. Tags: create FIRST via the GHL skill
🔗 How this connects to the AI Workforce Blueprint (Skill 23)

(only if sitemap unavailable; cap at 100 URLs)
Pseudocode — actual cron management uses openclaw cron commands

Step 1: Create the coupon (or reuse existing)
Step 2: Create a customer-facing promotion code

Step 9.30 — Install Customer Service & Support Playbooks (dual-mode) (v5.12)

Adjustments to consider (no action without your approval)

Minor improvements (agent can apply with approval)
Step 9.36 — Install Model Version Freshness Checker (v5.14)
✅ Checkpoint F — Advanced features active (Phase 5 complete)
Step 10 — Create the Agent Capabilities Playbook
Phase-completion verification (cross-check checkpoints A through G)
DOC + BACKEND READINESS (completion gates — testing happens only AFTER both pass)
Part 2 — The Client Reference Sheet (what gets written to Notion or the markdown file)
Part 3 — Six channel-specific Raw Body JSONs
Part 4 — The agent's final chat summary (after QC passes)
Part 5 — Safety rules (the agent must not violate these)
Part 6 — Operator notes (for the operator, not for clients)
